title: Universal Health Credential Registration Opens for Mexicans 85+ — Phase 1 of 120M Enrollment
detail: Mexico launched the first phase of its Universal Health Credential registration on April 13, 2026, beginning with citizens aged 85 and older at over 2,059 Welfare Ministry modules across the country. The credential — created by presidential decree on April 8 — entitles all 120 million Mexicans to access any public health facility (IMSS, ISSSTE, or IMSS-Bienestar) regardless of prior enrollment. Registration continues through April 30, organized by age group and surname initial, with a dedicated mobile app and AI-assisted digital consultations to follow. Full institutional integration is targeted for January 1, 2027. From 2027, the credential will also serve as national official identification. The enrollment requirement is: official ID, certified CURP, proof of address within six months, and a contact phone number. International progressive media framed the initiative as a landmark expansion of universal healthcare, noting the contrast with the U.S. system under the Trump administration.

media.caption: Mexico officially launches universal healthcare credential registration for 120 million citizens

title: Sheinbaum Announces School Supply Support for 7.2M Elementary Students; Education Programs at Record Highs
detail: During her April 13 mañanera press conference, President Sheinbaum announced that over 7.2 million elementary school students would receive economic support for school supplies and uniforms for the 2026–27 school year. The president also highlighted record program enrollment across the administration's education initiatives: 8.2 million beneficiaries in the Beca Rita Cetina scholarship program, 4.1 million in the Beca Benito Juárez secondary program, and 423,000 in Jóvenes Escribiendo el Futuro higher education grants. Sheinbaum addressed the upcoming Barcelona trip, reaffirming that Mexico-Spain diplomatic relations had never formally been severed despite AMLO's earlier estrangement, and that the visit would signal renewed engagement with multilateral institutions. She also noted that Mexico's constitutional sovereignty clause — protecting against unauthorized foreign intervention — was already being drafted for submission to Congress.

media.caption: Sheinbaum announces 7.2M school supply support at April 13 mañanera
